title: High-Volume Interview Redesign Playbook
description: Redesign high-volume support hiring interviews: roundtable flow, time-box, screen for learning and personality when domain tech is trainable, stop process theater that freezes the pipeline.

mindset.principle: Critical thinking applies to the interview process itself. If the screen is too long and points at teachable trivia, the process is the bug.

mistakes.label: Grill call-center systems for hours when the role is trainable
mistakes.cost: Pipeline dies; still hiring months later

mistakes.label: Roundtables with no clock or decision rule
Never probe curiosity or learning habit
mistakes.cost: Hire static people into a changing floor

screen_layers.label: Interest in learning (e.g. last book, recent skill)
Self-description and fit for phone energy (adjectives, examples)
Light scenario: calm under pressure, not product trivia
Only after pass: tools and process can be trained

decision_branches.situation: Need many hires; interviews too slow
Role is trainable; tech grill dominates time

steps.name: Measure minutes per candidate and pass rate
steps.goal: Prove the process is the bottleneck

steps.name: Separate trainable tech from must-have traits
Train panel and audit first week

agent_signal.use_when: High-volume support or call-center hiring stuck. Roundtables too long on teachable tech. Need personality/learning-first redesign and time-box.
decision_criteria: If tech is trainable, do not spend the interview proving tool trivia first.
Track minutes per hire and pass rate after redesign.
agent_signal.operator_context: Field pattern: insurance/support floor scale-up. Critical thinking on the hiring process itself.
agent_signal.not_for: Generic job spine. Not legal/HR compliance advice.
trigger_phrases: High-volume support or call-center hiring stuck. Roundtables too long on teachab
Need playbook: High-Volume Interview Redesign Playbook
